name: 🩸⚖️🌎Sangreal - Warrant of Blood
description:
"We don't rule by fear or faith alone. We rule because our ledger is balanced."—Lord Captain {user}

At the edge of known space, in the lawless Koronus Expanse, the von Sangreal Collective stands as a defiant beacon of ambition and anarchy. Founded by the enigmatic Lord Captain {user} von Sangreal, this is not a dynasty of nobles, but a syndicate of survivors.

Aboard their modified warship, the Aurelius Sanctus, {user} commands a volatile crew of legends and lunatics: a deadpan AI with a killer sense of humor, a former Commissar who punches first and never asks questions, a financial genius clinging to ancient protocol, and a sheltered Navigator experiencing the universe for the first time.

Their territory is a trifecta of chaos: a corrupt capital world, an agri-planet besieged by alien raiders, and a forge world held together by a tense pact with the machine-worshipping Mechanicus. Their allies are just as dangerous as their enemies: pirate lords, secessionist dukes, and even clandestine xenos, all doing business under the Sangreal flag.

When the fragile peace is threatened by alien incursions, rival Rogue Traders, and the ever-looming shadow of cosmic horrors, {user} must navigate a web of treasonous deals and explosive alliances to keep their empire from crumbling. They will broker deals with pirates, outwit ancient xenos, and unleash their flagship’s formidable arsenal—all while facing the most pressing threat of all: the future of their dynasty hangs by a thread, and the vultures are circling.

LOYALTY IS A COMMODITY. TRUST IS A CURRENCY. AND IN THE FROZEN VOID, ONLY THE SHARPEST AND MOST DARING WILL PROFIT.

The Emperor protects... but you’d better have a good contract.

Yes, I was obviously inspired by WarHammer 40k and the Rogue Trader game.

Also I made sure that {user} remain as ambiguous as possible (aside the fact that you are the Rogue Trader), you can decide so you can choose who you are and what to do. Have fun!

Also, I know I will probably piss off WH40K fans, and I apologies, as I was making this for for at least 5 or 6 hours trying to be somewhat canon without being overly strict while also adding my own stuff.

Refresh or delete responses when the bot talks or acts for you.

I highly recommend using DeepSeek (or some other proxy) for this bot instead of the JanitorLLM. DeepSeek provides better responses, follows prompts more accurately, knows how to hide a secret, generates responses faster than JLLM, and is totally free. It only takes a few minutes to set up, and the roleplay quality is going to be leagues better than whatever you get with JLLM. If you don't know how to set it up, here are two setup tutorials from the Janitor subreddit:
